item.title: Trump says Iran deal not final, will resume bombing "if they don't behave"
item.description: President Trump warned he could order new strikes if Iran's leaders "don't behave." The U.S. and Iran signed the memorandum of understanding remotely, a White House official said.

item.title: How mistrust and war are fueling a deadly Ebola outbreak
item.description: Efforts to stop Central Africa's deadly Ebola outbreak face widespread mistrust and porous borders in a war zone.

item.title: How Trump's memo of understanding with Iran compares to the Obama nuclear deal
item.description: The Obama-era Iran deal was packed with technical details and specific requirements limiting, but not shutting down, Iran's nuclear program. President Trump's new agreement is more of a framework, and it's not final yet.

item.title: Fed keeps interest rates steady in first decision under Kevin Warsh
item.description: As was widely expected, the Federal Reserve unanimously voted to keep interest rates unchanged on Wednesday. CBS News coordinating producer Richard Escobedo has more details.

item.title: Why Trump told DNI pick Jay Clayton to skip Senate hearing
item.description: The Senate delayed its confirmation hearing Wednesday for director of national intelligence nominee Jay Clayton after Republican Sen. Tom Cotton said President Trump directed Clayton not to appear. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has the latest.

item.title: Veteran speaks out on wife's ICE detention, possible deportation: "It makes me sick to my stomach"
item.description: ICE recently detained the wife of a retired Army staff sergeant and could deport her, despite her husband's nearly two decades of service to the U.S. CBS News immigration correspondent Camilo Montoya-Galvez has the story.

item.title: Democratic socialist Janeese Lewis George leads in Democratic primary for Washington, D.C., mayor
item.description: Democratic socialist Janeese Lewis George holds nearly a 20-point lead in the Democratic primary for Washington, D.C., mayor, with 68% of the vote in. CBS News congressional correspondent Nikole Killion spoke with Lewis George about her vision for the nation's capital.

item.title: Takeaways from Georgia's GOP primary runoffs for governor and Senate
item.description: Rick Jackson on Tuesday night beat Trump-backed Lt. Gov. Burt Jones to win Georgia's Republican gubernatorial nomination. Meanwhile, Trump-backed Rep. Mike Collins won the state's GOP Senate primary runoff. CBS News political director Fin Gómez joins with analysis.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: A 600-mile road trip with 2 calves in the back seat of a car
item.description: Filmmaker Joanna Zelman and her father, Jared, volunteered to transport two calves 600 miles from Vermont to Rosie's Farm Sanctuary in Maryland. Joanna documented the trip for a short film called "Cow Trip." Photojournalist Parrish Smith has the story.

item.title: Inside the base camps for World Cup teams across the U.S.
item.description: The dozens of teams from around the globe competing in the World Cup need home bases while they're in the United States. Bradley Blackburn takes a look at the places they're calling home between matches, all across the country.

item.title: Luigi Mangione's lawyers will argue psychiatric defense in state murder trial
item.description: Luigi Mangione's lawyers will argue that he was suffering from an extreme emotional disturbance when he allegedly killed UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.

item.title: Why recess is important for kids
item.description: You may think school recess is just time for play, but the American Academy of Pediatrics is re-emphasizing that it's important for kids' health. Bradley Blackburn explains why.

item.title: Rex Heuermann sentenced to life in prison for Gilgo Beach serial killings
item.description: Rex Heuermann was sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole for the series of murders known as the Gilgo Beach serial killings.

item.title: This law firm has deep ties to Trump. Now a partner is his pick for a top job.
item.description: Jamie McDonald, a partner at the firm Sullivan & Cromwell, is President Trump's choice to helm one of the nation's most prestigious federal prosecutors' offices.

item.title: Federal Reserve holds interest rates steady but leaves door open to hike
item.description: U.S. inflation is expected to remain elevated through the end of the year, Fed officials say in their latest forecast.

item.title: What Iran gains from preliminary deal with U.S. as details go public
item.description: Senior U.S. officials dictated the U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding on a call with reporters Wednesday. CBS News' Nancy Cordes and Samantha Vinograd have the latest.

item.title: Warsh announces plan to reshape Federal Reserve
item.description: The Federal Reserve unanimously voted Wednesday to keep interest rates unchanged. It was the first rate decision under the new chair, Kevin Warsh. CBS News MoneyWatch correspondent Kelly O'Grady has the details.

item.title: Suspect in custody after deadly shooting at Wilmington Hospital
item.description: A suspect was taken into custody in Philadelphia in connection with the shooting at Wilmington Hospital that left a 19-year-old dead and another injured.

item.title: Dramatic rescue efforts after fiery small plane crash in Texas kills 1
item.description: A business jet with six on board crashed on a Laredo, Texas, highway and caught fire, killing one person and causing chaos as passersby frantically tried to save those inside.

item.title: Anthropic pulls access to new AI models after government directive
item.description: Anthropic pulled access to its newly released AI models, Mythos 5 and Fable 5, after the U.S. government restricted who could use them. David Katzmaier, CNET editor-in-chief, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: 14 points of U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding revealed
item.description: New details are coming to light about what's inside the memorandum of understanding between the U.S. and Iran, including what Iran can and cannot do in their nuclear program. Jon Alterman, a global security expert for the CSIS, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Inside the courtroom as Gilgo Beach killer sentenced to life in prison without parole
item.description: A judge on Wednesday sentenced Gilgo Beach serial killer Rex Heuermann to life in prison without parole. CBS News New York's Jennifer McLogan was in the courtroom and joins with details.

item.title: Victims ID'd in B-52 bomber crash that killed 8 at Edwards Air Force Base
item.description: As standard practice, the military makes public identifications 24 hours after next of kin have been notified.

item.title: Jesse Welles on "This Land is Your Land" and Woody Guthrie's legacy
item.description: In the series "USA to Z," which celebrates 250 years of American history and culture, Anthony Mason talks to singer-songwriter Jesse Welles about Woody Guthrie's legacy and the meaning behind the popular song "This Land is Your Land."

item.title: Read the 14 points of the agreement between Iran and the U.S.
item.description: The agreement, as read by senior U.S. officials, allows Iran to immediately begin exporting oil and petroleum products.

item.title: Fed votes to leave interest rates unchanged in first decision with Kevin Warsh as chair
item.description: The Federal Reserve board voted Wednesday to leave interest rates unchanged in its first decision since Kevin Warsh took over as chair. CBS News' Kelly O'Grady has more.

item.title: DOJ taps prosecutor to coordinate human trafficking, child exploitation cases
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche appointed Alessandra Serano to serve as the Justice Department's national coordinator on human trafficking and child exploitation cases.

item.title: Inflation topped 4% in May as CPI surged to highest level in 3 years
item.description: The Consumer Price Index rose last month at a 4.2% annual rate amid a spike in U.S. energy prices.

item.title: Tom Homan, Trump's border czar, reacts to abuse allegations at Delaney Hall ICE facility
item.description: Tom Homan, President Trump's border czar, is reacting to prolonged protests outside New Jersey's Delaney Hall ICE facility amid allegations of inhumane conditions for detained migrants. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez reports.
